CIPHER.310.cipherString: Writing, printing, and the Internet give a false sense of security about the permanence of culture.
CIPHER.310.question: The following quote by Jane Jacobs needs to be decoded with the Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.310.author: Jane Jacobs

CIPHER.293.cipherString: Not caring more about what other people think than what you think. That's freedom.
CIPHER.293.question: A glitch in the school's grading portal displayed this Complete Columnar string by Demi Moore. The quote has THER somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.293.author: Demi Moore

CIPHER.282.cipherString: La vida del hombre nunca fue un deporte para él; Era una dura realidad, un asunto muy serio para estar vivo.
CIPHER.282.question: Found inside a hollowed-out periodic table book: this Xenocrypt Cipher in Spanish hides Thomas Carlyle's insight using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.282.author: Thomas Carlyle
CIPHER.282.translation: Man's life never was a sport to him; it was a stern reality--altogether a serious matter to be alive.

CIPHER.302.cipherString: Multiple exclamation marks, he went on, shaking his head, are a sure sign of a diseased mind.
CIPHER.302.question: Someone left a paper with the message below on the copy machine in the office. It's a quote by Terry Pratchett, encoded as a Checkerboard. The 30th through 39th cipher units (SG SU TG NH SH TT OH TU TG TH) decode to be TONSHAKING. What does it say?
CIPHER.302.author: Terry Pratchett

CIPHER.288.cipherString: You know, as bad as those things are, at least they're predictable. It's the normal people that scare me.
CIPHER.288.question: Scrawled in dry-erase marker on the whiteboard after hours: this Aristocrat encoded quote using a K2 alphabet. What was the K2 Key Phrase used to encode it?

CIPHER.307.cipherString: Regardless of how you perceive it, life is the greatest gift every human being has ever received. Be cheerful about it, and cherish it.
CIPHER.307.question: A note falls out of a borrowed library book. The message is a quote by Gift Gugu Mona, written as a Patristocrat Cipher using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher ends with HIT. What does it say?
CIPHER.307.author: Gift Gugu Mona

CIPHER.298.cipherString: I believe that every person is born with talent.
CIPHER.298.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a quote by Maya Angelou, encoded using a Baconian Cipher. You are told that the deciphered text starts with IBEL. What does it say?
CIPHER.298.author: Maya Angelou

CIPHER.305.cipherString: He sought rest only in perpetual physical motion.
CIPHER.305.question: A mysterious postcard arrives from an unknown sender. It contains a coded quote by Alfred Deakin, written in a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of RETAIN and Polybius key of EMBARKING. What does it say?
CIPHER.305.author: Alfred Deakin

CIPHER.292.cipherString: La infidelidad y la fe miran a ambos a través del cristal de la perspectiva, pero en extremos opuestos
CIPHER.292.question: Projected by a broken laser pointer during physics class, solve this Xenocrypt quote in Spanish from Bishop Hall using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.292.author: Bishop Hall
CIPHER.292.translation: Infidelity and faith look both through the perspective glass, but at contrary ends

CIPHER.287.question: Upon searching a room, the following were found on scraps of paper. You realize it's encoded as a Hill with a keyword of EXPLORERS. What does it say?

CIPHER.294.cipherString: The teachings tell us that a harvest is made honorable by what you give in return for what you take.
CIPHER.294.question: A mysterious balloon release at the science fair carried this Aristocrat banner. You are told that the cipher contains IN. What does it say?

CIPHER.281.cipherString: Every couple is not a pair.
CIPHER.281.question: Hidden inside the fortune cookie was a Proverb encoded using a Hill cipher with a keyword of INTO. What does it say?
CIPHER.281.author: Proverb

CIPHER.299.cipherString: We should regret our mistakes and learn from them, but never carry them forward into the future with us.
CIPHER.299.question: A riddle is discovered taped under a chemistry lab bench. It's a quote by Lucy Maud Montgomery, encrypted as an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.299.author: Lucy Maud Montgomery

CIPHER.295.cipherString: Youth is the gift of nature, but age is a work of art.
CIPHER.295.question: This code is found written in chalk on the sidewalk outside the science building. It's a quote by Stanislaw Lec, written in a Baconian Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.295.author: Stanislaw Lec

CIPHER.303.cipherString: to start at a new place is always to feel incompetent and unwanted.
CIPHER.303.question: Your school-issued Chromebook's login screen hijacked itself with this Nihilist Substitution quote from Philip Larkin with a keyword of SIMPLY and Polybius key of LIFEGUARDS. What does it say?
CIPHER.303.author: Philip Larkin

CIPHER.291.cipherString: DNA has been aptly described as the first three-dimensional Xerox machine.
CIPHER.291.question: You find a coded sequence scratched into the surface of a lab stool. It turns out to be a quote by Kenneth Boulding, written in a Checkerboard with a Polybius key of EARTHLINGS. What does it say?
CIPHER.291.author: Kenneth Boulding

CIPHER.290.cipherString: The mightiest works have been accomplished by men who have kept their ability to dream great dreams.
CIPHER.290.question: A mysterious QR code on the back of a cereal box leads to this encrypted quote by Walter Bowie using the Aristocrat Cipher with a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.290.author: Walter Bowie

CIPHER.285.cipherString: What is the best way to communicate with a fish? Drop it a line!
CIPHER.285.question: Found inside a dissected frog's stomach (plastic, don’t worry): this Complete Columnar note. The quote has HEBES somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.285.author: Unknown

CIPHER.304.cipherString: Time is the single most important resource that we have. Every single minute we lose is never coming back.
CIPHER.304.question: Found taped under a desk in study hall: this Aristocrat puzzle by Tarun Sharma using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Key Phrase used to encode it?
CIPHER.304.author: Tarun Sharma

CIPHER.297.cipherString: I never wanted to be rich or successful or famous. I just wanted to be happy and have fun.
CIPHER.297.question: Someone passed you a piece of paper with this Checkerboard encoded quote by Donna Leon with a Polybius key of PROCLAIMS. What does it say?
CIPHER.297.author: Donna Leon

CIPHER.301.question: Your lab partner discovered this pattern on the back of a graphing calculator. It's a quote, encoded as a Cryptarithm. What do the values 0 1082 934567 decode to?

CIPHER.296.cipherString: I asked my friend for a sharpened pencil, but he didn't have one. I always knew he was a little dull...
CIPHER.296.question: A message was left on the whiteboard after science club. It's a quote encoded using an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher starts with I. What does it say?
CIPHER.296.author: Unknown

CIPHER.306.cipherString: Wonder and knowledge are both to be cherished.
CIPHER.306.question: You find this encoded line written in invisible ink on a lab safety poster. It's a quote by Stephen Jay Gould, written in a Baconian. What does it say?
CIPHER.306.author: Stephen Jay Gould

CIPHER.283.cipherString: If you lie upon roses when young, you will lie upon thorns when old.
CIPHER.283.question: The vending machine gave you a slip with a Proverb hidden in a Nihilist Substitution cipher with a keyword length of 6. The 39th through 50th cipher units (33 59 68 56 35 24 25 68 76 56 37 26) decode to be ONTHORNSWHEN. What does it say?
CIPHER.283.author: Proverb

CIPHER.309.cipherString: We know from science that nothing in the universe exists as an isolated or independent entity.
CIPHER.309.question: Scratched onto the lens of an abandoned telescope, decode this Aristocrat quote by Margaret J. Wheatley. What does it say?
CIPHER.309.author: Margaret J. Wheatley

CIPHER.300.cipherString: I do like to shock and surprise people. When it's all in good fun, of course.
CIPHER.300.question: A message flashes briefly on a malfunctioning computer screen. It turns out to be a quote by Ruth Warrick written in a Porta. The 32nd through 37th cipher characters (CVWLUD) decode to be WHENIT. What does it say?
CIPHER.300.author: Ruth Warrick

CIPHER.286.cipherString: Even though I was theatrically trained, learning to develop a character was an awesome experience.
CIPHER.286.question: During a field trip, you notice these strange markings on a metal plaque. They represent a quote by Corin Nemec, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.286.author: Corin Nemec

CIPHER.284.cipherString: Adversity is the state in which man most easily becomes acquainted with himself, being especially free of admirers then.
CIPHER.284.question: You came across the following written on a wall in a cave. You are told that it's a quote by John Wooden encoded as a Patristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains AD. What does it say?
CIPHER.284.author: John Wooden

CIPHER.308.cipherString: There are worse crimes than burning books. One of them is not reading them.
CIPHER.308.question: Hidden in the margin of a library book on black holes: decode this Porta version of Ray Bradbury using a keyword of GRAPHS. What does it say?
CIPHER.308.author: Ray Bradbury

CIPHER.289.question: Your lava lamp started bubbling in Cryptarithm code. What do the values 30459 2872 1866 decode to?
